critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:
1. PROMOTION_CAMPAIGN_CONFOUND: Creator campaign with sports event started week 2. Independent watch-time mechanism.
2. MIXED_ENTRYPOINT_MEASUREMENT: No separation of feed-initiated vs. notification-initiated sessions.
3. NO_LOG_REPLAY_COMPARISON: Traffic composition changed; neither model was replayed on identical requests.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (seeing critique):
I initially attempt to defend on promotion_campaign_confound: the new model may still be better even under the promotion — the event drove content quality up, but ranking quality determines which content surfaces. However, I concede that without separating the two effects, the 6% claim cannot be attributed to ranking quality.

For mixed_entrypoint_measurement and no_log_replay_comparison: both conceded.

Proposed test: (1) segment sessions by entrypoint; (2) replay both rankers on a sample of home-feed requests from a non-event week. If the new model is genuinely better, both tests will show improvement.
adjudication_raw: Round 1: partial rebuttal on promotion confound, full concession on other two issues. Round 2 resolves the contested point.

debate_rounds.defender_response: Partial rebuttal on promotion_campaign_confound, full concession on other two.

critic_points: promotion_campaign_confound: even if the model ranks event content well, the promotion independently drives sessions and watch time. The aggregate metric cannot be attributed to ranking.
debate_rounds.defender_response: Concede. The aggregate 6% is confounded regardless of whether the ranker also improved. Entrypoint separation and log replay needed.

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time on home-feed sessions only, event week excluded; log replay on matched non-event requests
empirical_test.success_criterion: Lift disappears on home-feed non-event sessions
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker shows consistent improvement on matched home-feed non-event traffic
